Topic: Playing Rock Band 1 during Rivals Break - Epic Talkies WTF?
Forum: Vocals Posted: Thu Apr 11, 2019 2:02 am Subject: Playing Rock Band 1 during Rivals Break - Epic Talkies WTF?
Typo.After typing 2,500 lines, there's bound to be a few. That's why I saved a copy of the text before erasing.
Also for the record the largest number of skips is 15 in Alive Live: Drop In The Park.
Update 03/22/19: This thread is no longer being used. See url=https://rockband.scorehero.com/forum/viewtopic.php?t=32287]this thread for submitting paths for both on disc and DLC from now on.
OK - Since the OP had finally grown to the point where I was no longer even allowed to edit anymore, something had to be done.
Why do you create an entirely new sheet when you update? Stops people from being able to bookmark it. Google automatically saves versions for you even if you just update it live.
This is missing something - it should either be 4/AS or 4/EwO but I didn't want to replay to find out. If anyone else does, pls post.

original text:
How the built environment affects physical activity and how changes in the environment can promote active living are issues that have captured public attention. There are many other links between health and the built environment, however, from air quality to food access. The Design for Health (DFH) project and its online presence, www.DesignforHealth.net, synthesizes the evidence base linking landscape, urban design, planning, and health.
Phase 1 of the Design for Health project took advantage of a specific situation in Minnesota. In the Twin Cities all municipalities update their plans on a 10-year cycle, mandated by the Metropolitan Council, with a deadline in 2008. Furthermore, the $286.4 billion transportation bill, SAFETEA-LU, provided over $3 billion exclusively to construct infrastructure for active living and active transportation. Of this $25 million had been earmarked for improvements in the Twin Cities. These events created a vital window of opportunity to integrate healthy-living principles into municipal plans and formed the impetus for Design for Health, Phase 1.
More information is available at DFH History and Activities.

National V.P. School Gallery | Launceston Then!
24 images of National V.P. Primary School and its replacement St. Catherines C of E School and all their pupils from the past.
The National School, St. Thomas Road. Photo by John Neale.
The National School building in 2014.
National School c.1920's with Charles Causley 2nd on the left in the middle row.
National School. Photo courtesy of Kathryn Hill.
National School 1953 Launceston Music Festival Winners trained by Miss Stanbury, pianist Charles Causley. Lennard Hodge, Barbara Watkins, Edward Hutchings, Mary Kerslake, John Maguire. Leonard Stanmore, Gwen Harris, Geoffrey Parish, Mary Lake, Myrtle Stephens. Photo Courtesy of Beryl Parish.
National School Boys Choir in 1954.
National V.P. School boys choir who won the Curtis Cup for best choir at the 1954 Launceston Music Festival.
National V.P. School Choir in 1954/55. Photo courtesy of Tarry Barriball.
A group of children from the National School visit the Launceston Fanciers Show in the Town Hall in 1959.
National School Maypole Dancers with the Mayor and Mayores of Launceston, Tasmania in 1961.
National School Choir in 1962.
National School Choir in 1964.
National School in 1962. .
National School Nativity play from 1967-68. Photo courtesy of Naomi Scudamore.
Article on the construction of St. Catherines School from May 1968.
St. Catherines School. Photo courtesy of Kathryn Martin.
St. Catherines School 1978. Photo courtesy of Paddy Curtis.
St. Catherines 76-77 Photo courtesy of Nick Dyne. Back Row:Mrs Beer, Peter Wyatt,Karl Hockridge,Johnny Phillpotts,Nick Dyne,Andy Bridges,Paul Jones,Neil Partridge,Martin Baker,Tracy Trevalyon,Tonya Keat,Graham Burden,middle row- Mark Symons,Steven Hancock,Ian Clements,Steven Binding,David Tout,Caroline Nut(?),Tanya Bartlett,Tracy Pengelly,Jo Matthews,Alison Taylor,Karen Burge,Ian Gilbert.
